headline: Link building · ranked by effort to result
standfirst: The Month 2 link-building plan for cmsfinancial.ae. Built on the June 4 backlink audit (529 RDs, 87% under AS 10, 60 disavow candidates, frycu.com PBN cluster). This chapter is the deliverable for Task 22 (Link-building strategy development, 3h), Task 23 (Link-building campaign execution, 4h), and the outreach component of Task 24 (Backlink audit, 2h). Sequenced from cheapest free wins to paid editorial, with templates ready to send.
summary_lead: Authority Score is the central problem. AS 8 today. Target: lift to 18–25 by 90 days through stacked free wins (regulator portals, broker directories, GCC media) plus 6–10 editorial placements. Disavow filing ships in Week 1 to stop the PBN drag. Outreach calendar locked, templates ready, prioritization below.

win_tiles.label: Starting point
win_tiles.tip: Current Semrush Authority Score. Captures the cumulative trust signal from the backlink profile.

win_tiles.label: 90-day target
win_tiles.tip: Realistic 90-day Authority Score range. Lift driven by disavow cleanup + 6–10 high-quality editorial placements + free regulator/directory listings.

win_tiles.label: Disavow filing
win_tiles.tip: 60 toxic referring domains identified by Task 24 audit, ready to disavow. Stops the PBN drag from frycu.com cluster and similar low-AS sources.

win_tiles.label: Free wins (90d)
win_tiles.tip: Regulator portals (CMA, DED, SCA), broker directories (BrokerChooser, ForexBrokers.com, BrokersView), and CMS Financial-owned channels (Linktree, Facebook, Instagram, LinkedIn, YouTube).

win_tiles.label: Editorial placements (90d)
win_tiles.tip: GCC business media (Khaleej Times, Gulf News, The National, Arabian Business), broker review desks (Investopedia, Forbes Advisor), and finance-niche guest posts.

win_tiles.label: Outreach list locked
win_tiles.tip: 23 high-priority targets bucketed: 8 Tier-0 (free regulator/directory), 10 Tier-1 (editorial outreach), 5 Tier-2 (paid placement). Templates ready below.

playbook.title: Sequence — fastest wins first
playbook.subtitle: Three tiers, executed in order. Tier 0 ships in Week 1 with zero budget.

targets.tactic: Upload disavow.txt via GSC Disavow Tool — 60 domains from the Task 24 audit (frycu.com cluster, Moldova low-AS hosts, off-topic recycled domains).
targets.expected_impact: Stops AS drag; should add ~1–2 AS points within 6 weeks as Google reprocesses the link graph.

targets.tactic: Confirm cmsfinancial.ae is correctly listed on the UAE Capital Market Authority's licensed firms register. Add the canonical URL if missing.
targets.expected_impact: Government .gov.ae domain. AS 80+. Highest possible trust signal for YMYL finance.

targets.tactic: Verify the DED trade name (registration #561501) public listing includes the website URL.
Claim and complete the CMS Financial profile on BrokerChooser. Add all licences, account types, fees, platform info.
targets.expected_impact: AS 70+. High traffic in broker-research queries.

targets.tactic: Same flow. Add platform support (MT5), instruments (2,000+), regulators (CMA).
Claim profiles, respond to reviews, link homepage from the verified profile.
Already a top-3 RD source. Push more traffic + canonical homepage citation. Refresh to point at /trading-platform/ + /account-comparison/.
Software / service marketplace claims where CMS Financial qualifies as a trading-platform-adjacent listing.
targets.expected_impact: Diversified RD profile from non-finance sources.

tiers.label: Editorial outreach (Week 3–8 · light budget for travel/expo)

targets.tactic: Pitch a CMA-licensing explainer: 'What CMA regulation actually protects UAE traders against'. Position CMS Financial as the data source.
targets.expected_impact: AS 78. Local-authority signal. High brand value.

targets.tactic: Op-ed pitch: '22 years of UAE forex trading — what changed and what didn't' (founder commentary).
Awards angle — Best Retail Broker (Forex Expo Dubai 2025) press release picked up with full credit.
Roundup contribution: 'How GCC traders are adapting to AI-search-driven research'. Provide stats from the Task 18 deploy.
Multiple finance-trade press outlets will cover the November event. Coordinate with the Expo PR team to be quoted in 3–5 recap pieces.
Already linking (moneyexpoindia.com). Pitch a follow-up piece or sponsored panel for next Mumbai edition.
Submit broker profile to Investopedia's broker-review desk. Long form review process but high authority outcome.
Pitch as a featured CMA-regulated broker for their UAE roundup. Provide founder commentary + photos.
Trade-press niche. Awards-driven outreach.
Already a top-3 RD (499 backlinks). Confirm relationship is legit, ask for a featured-broker upgrade on their platform.
Run 3–5 paid placements on finance-niche editorial sites (AS 50+). Vendor: Outreach.com or similar verified network.
PRNewswire or Business Wire wire-out on the next milestone (10-year anniversary, new licence, new product). Picks up 30–50 nofollow citations + 5–10 dofollow.
Higher-tier sponsorship gets organic links from the expo site + partner sites + recap articles.
Paid upgrade from claimed listing to featured broker. Higher visibility in their broker-comparison tables.
targets.expected_impact: Same RD as Tier-0 claim, but better traffic and authority pass.

targets.target: Niche broker review desk paid placement
targets.tactic: Decide vendor (FXLeaders, FXEmpire, Investing.com). Paid reviews where transparency complies with their guidelines.

templates.title: Outreach templates · copy and adapt
templates.subtitle: Two send-ready templates per tier. Personalize the [bracketed] fields.

items.name: T0 — Regulator listing update
items.use_for: CMA broker register, DED trade name, SCA portal
items.subject: CMS Financial Services LLC — request to update licensed firms listing
items.body:
Dear [Compliance/Listings team],

CMS Financial Services LLC (Licence 20200000144, Trading Broker in International Markets and OTC Derivatives) requests a review of our public listing on the [CMA broker register / DED trade name registry / SCA portal] to confirm the public website link is current.

Canonical URL: https://cmsfinancial.ae/
Licence references: 20200000144 + 20200000223
DED registration: 561501
Registered office: 1403, International Business Tower, Business Bay, Dubai

If any update is required from our side, please indicate the form/portal and we will action it within 5 business days.

Thank you,
[Sender · CMS Financial · phone · email]

items.name: T1 — Editorial pitch (op-ed)
items.use_for: Khaleej Times, Gulf News, The National, Arabian Business
items.subject: Op-ed pitch · 22 years of UAE forex — what regulation actually protects against
items.body:
Hi [Editor],

I'm with CMS Financial, a CMA-licensed UAE broker that has been operating from Business Bay since 2004. Across 22 years we have watched the regulatory environment for UAE retail traders evolve from largely offshore to fully onshore.

With the recent uptick in retail interest from AI-driven personal-finance content, there is a story worth covering: what CMA regulation actually protects UAE traders against — and what it does not. I would like to propose a 700–900 word op-ed under our CEO/founder byline, with original data we have collected from our own client base.

Happy to share an outline or full draft, your preference. Hard data, clear angle, no jargon.

Thanks for considering,
[Sender · CMS Financial]

items.name: T1 — Broker review desk submission
items.use_for: Investopedia, Forbes Advisor, BrokerChooser featured listing
items.subject: CMS Financial broker review submission — CMA-regulated UAE forex broker, 22 years
items.body:
Hi [Review desk],

Submitting CMS Financial for review on [publication]. We are a UAE-based CMA-licensed broker (licences 20200000144 + 20200000223), operating since 2004 from Dubai's Business Bay.

Key numbers for your fact-check:
- 2,000+ tradable instruments across forex, commodities, indices, Stock CFDs
- Platform: MetaTrader 5 (desktop, web, iOS, Android)
- Account tiers: Core ($0 min, spreads from 1.0 pip, 1:500 leverage), Master ($5K min, spreads from 0.6, 1:200), Signature ($10K min, raw spreads from 0, $5 commission/side, 1:100)
- Awards: Best Retail Broker (Forex Expo Dubai 2025), Best Multi-Asset Broker MENA, Best Trading Execution, Great Place to Work UAE 2025
- Languages: English + Arabic, 24/7 support
- Restricted jurisdictions: US, Turkey, UAE-sanctioned countries

Full press kit, screenshots, and founder availability for interview attached.

Thanks,
[Sender]

items.name: T2 — Paid guest post brief
items.subject: Paid editorial brief — UAE forex broker authority piece
items.body:
Brief:
- Target topic: 'How CMA-regulated UAE brokers differ from offshore alternatives — what retail traders need to verify'
- Word count: 1,200–1,500
- Author byline: CMS Financial (CEO or research head, name TBC)
- Tone: educational, regulation-led, neutral on competitors
- Required links: cmsfinancial.ae/ (do-follow, brand anchor), cmsfinancial.ae/regulated/ (do-follow, 'CMA licence' anchor)
- Compliance: no exaggerated returns claims, no comparative ranking statements
- Lead time: 14 business days from brief acceptance

prose.title: Why this sequence works.
paragraphs: Fix the leak before you turn on the tap. The frycu.com cluster (2,177 backlinks from a single PBN host on the same IP as the live site) is currently dragging on Authority Score. The disavow filing ships Week 1 — zero budget, ~15 minutes of work via GSC's Disavow Tool — and the ~1–2 point AS lift that follows is the cheapest impact in the entire plan.

Tier 0 is all free. The regulator portals (CMA, DED, SCA), the broker directories (BrokerChooser, ForexBrokers, BrokersView, TrustPilot), and the owned-channel cleanup (Linktree, social profiles) can all ship in the first two weeks with no spend. Combined, they add 6–8 high-quality referring domains — including the highest-trust possible: government .gov.ae citations.

Tier 1 is the editorial campaign. 22 years of operation + CMA licensing + 8 awards is a strong PR hook. The pitches in the playbook are tuned to outlets that already cover this beat. Realistic conversion: 4–6 placements across the 10 outreach targets over 60 days. Each placement is worth 4–6 of the low-AS Tier-0 listings combined.

Tier 2 needs an approval call. Paid placement networks and press wire distributions cost real budget ($3K–$6K total recommended). This tier only ships once budget is confirmed. It accelerates the AS lift but isn't the foundation — Tier 0 + Tier 1 alone get to the 18-AS floor of the 90-day target.

pull_quote.text: Tier 0 ships in two weeks with zero budget. Tier 1 is a 60-day editorial campaign. Tier 2 waits on a budget call.
